title: pelargonium rungvense
intro: Pelargonium rungvense, also known as the Umckaloabo plant, is a species of succulent that belongs to the geranium family, commonly found in the Kalahari Desert. The plant has been traditionally used in South African medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly for its ability to reduce inflammation and fight off infections. The health benefits of Pelargonium rungvense include relief from cold and flu symptoms, reduced respiratory issues, and improved digestion, making it a popular herbal remedy. The plant is often prepared into teas, capsules, or tinctures, and is also used topically to treat skin conditions and wounds.
